content: A hybrid planning and learning system can be re-framed as a modular 'ecosystem' of nested semi-autonomous curious micro-agents, where each micro-agent handles exploration of a single sub-planning branch, rather than the entire system being controlled by a single monolithic policy. This architecture leverages each micro-agent's intrinsic curiosity to actively prioritize high-uncertainty subproblems for memory retention, automatically pruning low-value branches during the planning phase.
